name: The Simpsons
description: Springfield's first family and their yellow antics

questions.q: What is Bart Simpson's full name?

source.notes: An anagram of 'brat.' Born on April 1, 1979 (per show canon).

questions.q: What does the 'J' in Homer J. Simpson stand for?

source.notes: Revealed in Season 2. Named as a tribute to Bullwinkle's 'Jay Ward.'

questions.q: What was Marge Simpson's maiden name?

source.notes: Like Jackie Kennedy's maiden name. Her twin sisters Patty and Selma are also Bouviers.

questions.q: What is the name of the Simpson family's dog?

source.notes: A former racing greyhound. Introduced in the first full episode (1989).

questions.q: What is the first name of Bart's best friend?

source.notes: Named after President Richard Milhous Nixon, creator Matt Groening's least favorite president.

questions.q: In which fictional American town do the Simpsons live?

source.notes: The state is deliberately ambiguous, though creator confirmed Springfield, Oregon inspired it.

questions.q: What is the name of the bar where Homer frequently drinks?

source.notes: Owned by Moe Szyslak. Address is 57 Walnut Street.

questions.q: What is the nefarious exclamation of Mr. Burns when he's pleased with an evil scheme?

source.notes: Owner of the Springfield Nuclear Power Plant. Pure evil with 104 years on Earth.

questions.q: Who shot Mr. Burns in the two-part mystery episode?

source.notes: Season 6-7 cliffhanger. Maggie shot him accidentally when Burns tried to steal her lollipop.

questions.q: Who is the Simpsons' next-door neighbor, known for his moustache and cheerful phrases like 'Howdy-diddly-doo'?

source.notes: Devout Christian. Named after Flanders Street in Portland, Oregon.

questions.q: Who is the creator of 'The Simpsons'?

source.notes: Created the shorts in 1987 on 'The Tracey Ullman Show.' Half-hour series premiered 1989.

questions.q: Which actor voices Sideshow Bob, Bart's recurring vengeful nemesis?

source.notes: Grammer is most famous for 'Frasier.' Has won an Emmy for the Sideshow Bob role.

questions.q: Which voice actor provides the voice of Homer Simpson?

source.notes: Also voices Krusty the Clown, Grampa Simpson, Mayor Quimby, Barney, and Groundskeeper Willie.

questions.q: Which voice actress brings Bart Simpson's iconic voice to life?

source.notes: Originally auditioned for Lisa (played by Yeardley Smith). Has voiced Bart since the shorts began in 1987.

questions.q: What is the principal of Springfield Elementary named?

source.notes: Revealed to actually be 'Armin Tamzarian,' an imposter who assumed the real Skinner's identity.

questions.q: What is the rival town to Springfield that the Simpsons constantly mock?

source.notes: Founded by Shelbyville Manhattan, who broke away from Jebediah Springfield.

questions.q: What dietary choice does Lisa Simpson adopt in Season 7?

source.notes: From the episode 'Lisa the Vegetarian' (1995). Paul and Linda McCartney guest starred.

questions.q: What color is the Simpsons' family couch in the famous opening 'couch gag'?

source.notes: Iconic opening sequence element since Season 1. Each gag is different.

questions.q: Which Simpsons character's catchphrase is 'Eat my shorts'?

source.notes: One of Bart's iconic catchphrases along with 'Ay Caramba!' and 'Don't have a cow, man.'

questions.q: In what year did 'The Simpsons' first air as a half-hour series on Fox?

source.notes: December 17, 1989. First episode: 'Simpsons Roasting on an Open Fire.' Longest-running American sitcom.
